div1.entryFree:
Beak, 1) the sharp and crooked bill of a bird of prey: Ven. 56. Lucr. 508. H6B III, 2, 193. Cymb. V, 4, 118.
2) bill in general: turn their halcyon --s with every gale, Lr. II, 2, 84.
3) the forecastle of a ship: now on the b., now in the waist, the deck, in every cabin, I flamed amazement, Tp. I, 2, 196.
